step_by_step_thinking: I agree that option D (80-100 units/kg every 6 hours) is the best choice based on current guidelines for managing Hemophilia B peri-operatively. More frequent dosing like this helps ensure continuous maintenance of sufficient factor IX levels necessary to safely manage intraparenchymal blood loss without excessive clotting risks associated with lower frequencies such as once daily doses. Additionally, studies have shown better outcomes from prophylactic infusions spaced closer together than wider intervals between treatments. Therefore, my final selection remains D.
